_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
procedure_name: Mammary duct fistula - Total duct excision
source_files: Mammary duct fistula - Total duct excision_Medco.pdf
Mammary duct fistula - Total duct excision_PPD.CPD.pdf
clinical_indications: Recurrent inflammation – redness, heat, pain, edema

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication for procedure, and planned line of management

claim.label: Detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) with treatment details

ppd_preauth.q: Are clinical notes with detailed history, signs & symptoms, planned line of treatment, and indication for procedure available?
Did Ultrasound chest report confirm the diagnosis?
Was clinical evaluation and imaging indicative of surgery?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advice at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Clinical evaluation and imaging not indicative of surgery for mammary duct fistula.
Ultrasound report for the affected site did not confirm the diagnosis.
Clinical notes are missing or incomplete regarding evaluation findings, indication for procedure, or planned line of management.
Histopathological examination report was not submitted.
Detailed Procedure / Operative Notes are missing or incomplete.

clinical_key_pointers: Mammary fistula is an abnormal communication of the mammary ducts with the skin, being included in the group of chronic breast suppurations, a pathology characterized by numerous recurrences, apparently without cause, which raises problems for both the patient and the surgeon.
Subareolar abscess resulting from duct ectasia and periductal mastitis are the major predisposing factors.
